sourceMovie.title: Eden Lake
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Eden Lake for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. James Watkins keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 28 Years Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eden Lake clicked for you, 28 Years Later (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eden Lake?
faq.answer: Try 28 Years Later, The Lost Boys, The Last House on the Left—each shares DNA with Eden Lake while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eden Lake?
faq.answer: Eden Lake (2008) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eden Lake worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
